creator: joed@thebride.london.silverplatter.com
text:
When booting without a CD-ROM disc in the SCSI the boot
	system halts when trying to mount /cdrom, and the user is 
	given the option of entering a sysmadmin shell. I don't 
	believe an empty CD-ROM drive should prevent the system booting.

Fix: 

This was fixed with the addition of the no-auto flags.
How-To-Repeat: 
	Reboot with an empty CD drive.
